adaptedResources.author: Microsoft Corporation
adaptedResources.description: The `Text` DSC resource is used to manage the Windows text size accessibility setting.

Size.title: Size
Size.description:
The desired text size. Accepted values are `Small`, `Medium`, `Large`, or `ExtraLarge`.
        This is a key property. Defaults to `KeepCurrentValue`.

SizeValue.title: SizeValue
SizeValue.description: A read-only property indicating the current numeric text scale factor. This property is not configurable.

embedded.description: The `Text` DSC resource is used to manage the Windows text size accessibility setting.
embedded.title: Microsoft.Windows.Setting.Accessibility/Text

adaptedResources.author: Microsoft Corporation
adaptedResources.description: The `Magnifier` DSC resource is used to manage Windows Magnifier accessibility settings.

Magnification.title: Magnification
Magnification.description:
The magnification preset to apply. Accepted values are `None`, `Low`, `Medium`, or `High`.
        This is a key property. Defaults to `KeepCurrentValue`.

ZoomIncrement.title: ZoomIncrement
ZoomIncrement.description: The zoom increment percentage. This is a mandatory property. Defaults to `25`.

StartMagnify.title: StartMagnify
StartMagnify.description: Specifies whether to start the Magnifier application after applying settings. Defaults to `$false`.

MagnificationLevel.title: MagnificationLevel
MagnificationLevel.description: A read-only property indicating the current magnification level in percent. This property is not configurable.

ZoomIncrementLevel.title: ZoomIncrementLevel
ZoomIncrementLevel.description: A read-only property indicating the current zoom increment value. This property is not configurable.

embedded.description: The `Magnifier` DSC resource is used to manage Windows Magnifier accessibility settings.
embedded.title: Microsoft.Windows.Setting.Accessibility/Magnifier

adaptedResources.author: Microsoft Corporation
adaptedResources.description: The `MousePointer` DSC resource is used to manage the Windows mouse pointer size accessibility setting.

PointerSize.title: PointerSize
PointerSize.description:
The desired mouse pointer size. Accepted values are `Normal`, `Medium`, `Large`, or `ExtraLarge`.
        This is a key property. Defaults to `KeepCurrentValue`.

PointerSizeValue.title: PointerSizeValue
PointerSizeValue.description: A read-only property indicating the current pointer size value. This property is not configurable.

embedded.description: The `MousePointer` DSC resource is used to manage the Windows mouse pointer size accessibility setting.
embedded.title: Microsoft.Windows.Setting.Accessibility/MousePointer

adaptedResources.author: Microsoft Corporation
adaptedResources.description: The `VisualEffect` DSC resource is used to manage Windows visual accessibility settings.

SID.title: SID
SID.description: The security identifier. This is a key property and should not be set manually.

AlwaysShowScrollbars.title: AlwaysShowScrollbars
AlwaysShowScrollbars.description: Specifies whether scrollbars should always be visible.

TransparencyEffects.title: TransparencyEffects
TransparencyEffects.description: Specifies whether transparency effects are disabled (set `$true` to disable transparency).

MessageDurationInSeconds.title: MessageDurationInSeconds
MessageDurationInSeconds.description: The duration in seconds that notification messages are displayed. Must be between 5 and 300.

embedded.description: The `VisualEffect` DSC resource is used to manage Windows visual accessibility settings.
embedded.title: Microsoft.Windows.Setting.Accessibility/VisualEffect

adaptedResources.author: Microsoft Corporation
adaptedResources.description: The `Audio` DSC resource is used to manage Windows audio accessibility settings.

SID.title: SID
SID.description: The security identifier. This is a key property and should not be set manually.

EnableMonoAudio.title: EnableMonoAudio
EnableMonoAudio.description: Specifies whether mono audio should be enabled. Defaults to `$false`.

RestartService.title: RestartService
RestartService.description: Specifies whether to restart the Windows Audio service after applying changes. Defaults to `$false`.

embedded.description: The `Audio` DSC resource is used to manage Windows audio accessibility settings.
embedded.title: Microsoft.Windows.Setting.Accessibility/Audio

adaptedResources.author: Microsoft Corporation
adaptedResources.description: The `TextCursor` DSC resource is used to manage Windows text cursor accessibility settings.

SID.title: SID
SID.description: The security identifier. This is a key property and should not be set manually.

IndicatorStatus.title: IndicatorStatus
IndicatorStatus.description: Enables or disables the text cursor indicator accessibility feature.

IndicatorSize.title: IndicatorSize
IndicatorSize.description: The size of the text cursor indicator. Must be between 1 and 20.

IndicatorColor.title: IndicatorColor
IndicatorColor.description: The color of the text cursor indicator as an integer value.

Thickness.title: Thickness
Thickness.description: The thickness of the text cursor. Must be between 1 and 20.

embedded.description: The `TextCursor` DSC resource is used to manage Windows text cursor accessibility settings.
embedded.title: Microsoft.Windows.Setting.Accessibility/TextCursor

adaptedResources.author: Microsoft Corporation
adaptedResources.description: The `StickyKeys` DSC resource is used to manage the Windows Sticky Keys accessibility feature.

SID.title: SID
SID.description: The security identifier. This is a key property and should not be set manually.

Active.title: Active
Active.description: Specifies whether Sticky Keys is currently active.

Available.title: Available
Available.description: Specifies whether Sticky Keys is available to be toggled.

HotkeyActive.title: HotkeyActive
HotkeyActive.description: Specifies whether the Sticky Keys keyboard shortcut (pressing Shift 5 times) is active.

ConfirmOnHotkeyActivation.title: ConfirmOnHotkeyActivation
ConfirmOnHotkeyActivation.description: Specifies whether a confirmation dialog is shown when Sticky Keys is activated via hotkey.

HotkeySound.title: HotkeySound
HotkeySound.description: Specifies whether a sound plays when Sticky Keys hotkey is used.

VisualIndicator.title: VisualIndicator
VisualIndicator.description: Specifies whether a visual indicator is shown when Sticky Keys is active.

AudibleFeedback.title: AudibleFeedback
AudibleFeedback.description: Specifies whether an audible tone plays when a modifier key is pressed.

TriState.title: TriState
TriState.description: Specifies whether modifier keys can be locked by pressing them twice.

TwoKeysOff.title: TwoKeysOff
TwoKeysOff.description: Specifies whether Sticky Keys is turned off when two keys are pressed simultaneously.

embedded.description: The `StickyKeys` DSC resource is used to manage the Windows Sticky Keys accessibility feature.
embedded.title: Microsoft.Windows.Setting.Accessibility/StickyKeys

adaptedResources.author: Microsoft Corporation
adaptedResources.description: The `ToggleKeys` DSC resource is used to manage the Windows Toggle Keys accessibility feature.

SID.title: SID
SID.description: The security identifier. This is a key property and should not be set manually.

Active.title: Active
Active.description: Specifies whether Toggle Keys is currently active.

Available.title: Available
Available.description: Specifies whether Toggle Keys is available to be toggled.

HotkeyActive.title: HotkeyActive
HotkeyActive.description: Specifies whether the Toggle Keys keyboard shortcut (holding Num Lock for 5 seconds) is active.

ConfirmOnHotkeyActivation.title: ConfirmOnHotkeyActivation
ConfirmOnHotkeyActivation.description: Specifies whether a confirmation dialog is shown when Toggle Keys is activated via hotkey.

HotkeySound.title: HotkeySound
HotkeySound.description: Specifies whether a sound plays when Toggle Keys hotkey is used.

VisualIndicator.title: VisualIndicator
VisualIndicator.description: Specifies whether a visual indicator is shown when Toggle Keys is active.

embedded.description: The `ToggleKeys` DSC resource is used to manage the Windows Toggle Keys accessibility feature.
embedded.title: Microsoft.Windows.Setting.Accessibility/ToggleKeys

adaptedResources.author: Microsoft Corporation
adaptedResources.description: The `FilterKeys` DSC resource is used to manage the Windows Filter Keys accessibility feature.

SID.title: SID
SID.description: The security identifier. This is a key property and should not be set manually.

Active.title: Active
Active.description: Specifies whether Filter Keys is currently active.

Available.title: Available
Available.description: Specifies whether Filter Keys is available to be toggled.

HotkeyActive.title: HotkeyActive
HotkeyActive.description: Specifies whether the Filter Keys keyboard shortcut (holding Right Shift for 8 seconds) is active.

ConfirmOnHotkeyActivation.title: ConfirmOnHotkeyActivation
ConfirmOnHotkeyActivation.description: Specifies whether a confirmation dialog is shown when Filter Keys is activated via hotkey.

HotkeySound.title: HotkeySound
HotkeySound.description: Specifies whether a sound plays when Filter Keys hotkey is used.

VisualIndicator.title: VisualIndicator
VisualIndicator.description: Specifies whether a visual indicator is shown when Filter Keys is active.

AudibleFeedback.title: AudibleFeedback
AudibleFeedback.description: Specifies whether an audible tone plays when a key is pressed or accepted.

embedded.description: The `FilterKeys` DSC resource is used to manage the Windows Filter Keys accessibility feature.
embedded.title: Microsoft.Windows.Setting.Accessibility/FilterKeys

adaptedResources.author: Microsoft Corporation
adaptedResources.description: The `EyeControl` DSC resource is used to enable or disable the Windows Eye Control accessibility feature.

Ensure.title: Ensure
Ensure.description:
Specifies whether Eye Control should be enabled (`Present`) or disabled (`Absent`).
        This is a key property.

embedded.description: The `EyeControl` DSC resource is used to enable or disable the Windows Eye Control accessibility feature.
embedded.title: Microsoft.Windows.Setting.Accessibility/EyeControl
